mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return periods with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output capacity that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances within the specified regime, or regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 36-month rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 3.0 percent year-over-year triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of high-inflation trigger instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals weak consumer demand relative to output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity constraints and margin pressure that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate below 45% during late-cycle periods, or regime classification yields no incremental predictive power over baseline models.
